title: Gel Quantification
purpose: Measure DNA and RNA band intensity, normalize samples against controls, and export quantitative gel analysis data compatible with Fiji/ImageJ workflows.
learningTime: Learn this tool in under 2 minutes
capabilities: CSV and Excel export with ROI metadata

steps: Click Target then Control on bands

tour.title: Upload gel images
tour.body: Add one or more gel images to your session. TIFF, PNG, and JPEG are supported.

tour.title: Image and data views
tour.body: Switch between the image workspace and the unified multi-gel results table.

tour.title: Create ROIs
tour.body: Click a band to place a Target ROI, then click the matching control band. Adjust inner and outer boxes in the ROI manager.

tour.title: Manage pairs
tour.body: Rename pairs, edit sample labels, and review per-band measurements and ratios.

tour.title: Export results
tour.body: Download CSV or Excel reports with corrected intensities, ratios, and ROI coordinates.

overview.purpose: Quantify gel electrophoresis band intensity using user-defined ROIs and background subtraction.
overview.useCase: Compare relative band abundance across lanes, normalize to a housekeeping control, and export publication-ready tables.
overview.inputs: Gel images (TIFF preferred for 16-bit; PNG/JPEG supported). Target and control band placements per lane pair.
overview.outputs: Per-lane corrected intensities, target/control ratios, CSV/Excel exports with ROI metadata.

workflow.title: Upload image
workflow.description: Add gel files via the sidebar. Multiple gels can be analyzed in one session.

workflow.title: Place Target ROI
workflow.description: In Image View, click the band of interest. Default inner (30×70) and outer (45×85) boxes are applied.

workflow.title: Place Control ROI
workflow.description: Click the reference band in the same lane pair. The tool computes background-corrected intensity.

workflow.title: Review data table
workflow.description: Open the Data Table tab to compare lanes, filter by gel, and edit sample labels.

workflow.title: Export
workflow.description: Export CSV or Excel for downstream analysis or lab records.

tips: Use consistent exposure and imaging settings across gels in a comparison set.
Avoid saturated bands — clipped pixels cannot be recovered quantitatively.
Keep ROI dimensions consistent across lanes using the template defaults.
Normalize to a stable control lane within each gel.
Display invert is visual only and does not change exported values.

faq.q: Why is my band intensity zero?
faq.a: The ROI may miss the band, or the background ring may fully subtract signal. Reposition the inner box over the band and ensure the outer box captures local background only.

faq.q: Why do two bands give similar values?
faq.a: Bands with similar loading and exposure often yield comparable corrected intensities. Verify ROI placement and check for saturation.
No. Invert is a display-only adjustment. All quantification uses the original decoded pixel data.
Yes. Upload multiple images, switch between them with the gel selector, and export a unified dataset.

items: Multi-gel dataset support with unified export
